lede: A look at September 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Marco Polo and Graham Taylor, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Parsons Green train bombing
events.desc: The Parsons Green bombing takes place in London.

events.title: United Nations
events.desc: The United Nations cedes Eritrea to Ethiopia.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The climax of the Battle of Britain, when the Luftwaffe launches its largest and most concentrated attack of the entire campaign.

events.title: Nazi Germany
events.desc: Nazi Germany adopts a new national flag bearing the swastika.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Tanks are used for the first time in battle, at the Battle of the Somme.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Confederate forces capture Harpers Ferry, Virginia.

events.title: HMS Whiting (1812)
events.desc: HMS Whiting runs aground on the Doom Bar.

events.title: French Revolutionary Wars
events.desc: French Revolutionary Wars: Arthur Wellesley (later Duke of Wellington) sees his first combat at the Battle of Boxtel during the Flanders Campaign.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British forces land at Kip's Bay during the New York Campaign.

births.name: Carlos Dávila
births.line: Chilean journalist and politician, President of Chile

births.name: Agatha Christie
births.line: English crime novelist, short story writer, and playwright

births.name: William Howard Taft
births.line: American lawyer, jurist, and politician, 27th President of the United States

births.name: Jean Renoir
births.line: French actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

deaths.text: Willy Messerschmitt, German engineer and academic, designed the Messerschmitt Bf 109
Rudolf Christoph Eucken, German philosopher and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Johann Karl Simon Morgenstern, German-Estonian philologist and academic

observances.text: Christian feast day: Saint Dominic in Soriano (formerly)
